call_params.prompt_last_msg_preview:
{'role': 'user', 'content': '
## Review Form
Below is a description of the questions you will be asked on the review form for each paper and some guidelines on what to consider when answering these questions.
When writing your review, please keep in mind that after decisions have been made, reviews and meta-reviews of accepted papers and opted-in rejected papers will be made public.

1. Summary: Briefly summarize the paper and its contributions. This is not the place to critique the paper;

result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The paper addresses a highly relevant problem: the disconnect between marginal benchmark improvements and real-world deployment feasibility, specifically focusing on computational overhead and hyperparameter fragility in adaptive attention architectures. While the core message aligns with modern calls for efficiency-aware and latency-critical evaluation, the execution lacks the mechanistic transparency and formalized evaluation protocols required for a top-tier venue. The authors report useful empirical observations (e.g., +63% training time, +52% memory, narrow LR window, FP16 incompatibility), but these remain descriptive rather than rigorously quantified. There is no projection-based diagnostic analysis (e.g., SVD decay of activation covariances, Fisher discriminant ratios, or covariance condition numbers) to explain why dynamic gating destabilizes the optimization landscape. Hyperparameter sensitivity is noted qualitatively rather than quantified via stability radii, Hessi
